label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the beach. First, I need to look at all the given information and see when Leslie was doing other activities. The key here is to find a time when Leslie wasn't doing anything else, right? Because if someone saw Leslie somewhere else, then they couldn't have been at the beach at the same time. So let's list out all the times Leslie was seen doing things: 1. Leslie woke up at 5am. That's just a start time, not an activity duration. So maybe they were up from 5am onward? 2. Emily saw Leslie in class from 5am to 9am. So during that whole time, Leslie was at school. 3. Anthony saw Leslie watching a movie from 9am to 11am. So after school, Leslie went to the theater. 4. Thomas saw Leslie buying cookies from 11am to 4pm. Wait, 11am to 4pm? That's a long time at the bakery. So from 11 a.m. until 4 p.m., Leslie was there. 5. Ashley saw Leslie fixing their computer at the electronics store from 4pm to 5pm. So right after the bakery, Leslie was at the store for an hour. 6. Susan saw Leslie walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm.
